title: Red Sox continue to get bad news on Roman Anthony’s recovery from lingering wrist injury
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony has been out since May 4 due to a wrist injury, with no substantial recovery progress.
Red Sox interim manager Chad Tracy noted Anthony's slow recovery as a setback for the team.
Anthony's recovery from torn wrist ligaments is expected to take 8-12 weeks, affecting his return timeline.

title: Celtics select St. John’s wing Dillon Mitchell with 40th pick in 2026 NBA Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics selected Dillon Mitchell from St. John's with the 40th pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.
Mitchell, a 6-foot-7 wing, played for Texas, Cincinnati, and St. John's during his college career.
He is known for strong defensive capabilities but has limited offensive skills due to shooting challenges.

title: Boy, 13, hospitalized after being found unresponsive in swimming pool at Beverly home
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 13-year-old boy was found unresponsive in a swimming pool at a Beverly home on Wednesday afternoon.
The boy received CPR from bystanders and first responders before being stabilized at Beverly Hospital.
He was then airlifted to a Boston hospital; the incident is being investigated by Beverly police.

title: Here’s where every tall ship will be docked for Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: More than 60 tall ships to dock in Boston's Charlestown, downtown, and Seaport for Sail Boston 250 festival.
Seaport District will host Italy's Amerigo Vespucci and Argentina's Libertad, among other tall ships.
Public viewing from July 12-15, 10 a.m. to 10 p.m.; boarding hours vary by ship and day.

title: Windhorst: ‘Very good chance’ Jaylen Brown isn’t on Celtics next season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown is reportedly involved in trade discussions with multiple NBA teams, according to ESPN's Shams Charania.
Brian Windhorst suggests a significant chance Brown will not be with the Celtics next season despite having three years left on his contract.
Brad Stevens confirmed Brown's importance but did not rule out trades as Boston missed acquiring Giannis Antetokounmpo.

title: WCVB weeknight meteorologist Mike Wankum will retire end of June, station announces
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mike Wankum, WCVB weeknight meteorologist, will retire on June 30 after more than 20 years at the station.
Cindy Fitzgibbon will take over Wankum’s weeknight forecasts, currently handling morning and noon weather on weekdays.
Kelly Ann Cicalese will transition to weekdays, succeeding Fitzgibbon’s current roles; weekend successor not yet announced.

title: 14 years later, man charged in death of 7-year-old in Central Mass.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Steven Stuart, 35, charged with murder for 2017 death of Jayden Carlson in Auburn, per Worcester County DA.
Stuart previously convicted in 2015 for 2012 assault on Jayden, served six to eight years in state prison.
Jayden Carlson sustained severe injuries at age 2, leading to complications and his death at age 7.

title: Is your favorite Boston bar packed with World Cup revelers? Here are some FIFA-free alternatives.
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Blossom Bar at Sichuan Garden in Brookline remains quiet, attracting few World Cup fans and offering a sports-free environment.
Grendel’s Den in Cambridge provides a World Cup-free experience, with no TV and a classic pub atmosphere.
Wusong Tiki Bar in Cambridge avoids airing World Cup games, recommending reservations a week in advance for weekends.

title: Man allegedly stole nearly $2,000 from Medford church donation boxes, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Michael Bagley, 60, allegedly stole nearly $2,000 from donation boxes at St. Joseph’s Parish and St. Francis Church.
Bagley faces charges including larceny and breaking into a depository; he was arraigned and pleaded not guilty.
Police suspect Bagley of similar thefts in surrounding cities and towns over the past month.

title: Celtics guard Derrick White is taking a basketball job with his alma mater at Colorado
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Derrick White is appointed as the first 'President of Basketball Strategy' at the University of Colorado.
White will assist in NIL, revenue sharing, athlete development, and team culture for Colorado basketball.
White donated $2 million to Colorado men's basketball and will serve as a program ambassador.
